health_details.gemini_analysis_summary: The battery exhibits terminal degradation characteristics following 1,847 cycles and extreme thermal abuse with peaks reaching 67°C. Sustained operation below 300V and high current throughput indicates severe internal resistance increase and potential structural compromise.

listing.title: Grade F - High Cycle EV Battery Core for Recycling
listing.description: End-of-life battery pack with 1847 cycles and evidence of thermal stress. Recommended for professional recyclers and material recovery only; not for second-life energy storage.

safety_risks.description: Peak temperature reached 67.0°C, exceeding safe operating limits.
safety_risks.mitigation: Immediate decommissioning; battery is unsuitable for second-life SMEs due to fire risk.

safety_risks.description: Thermal abuse: sustained temps above 60°C accelerate SEI layer growth and electrolyte decomposition.
Electrical stress risk: persistent current spikes up to 166.5A and voltage sagging to 280V.
safety_risks.mitigation: Do not attempt further charging cycles.

safety_risks.description: Voltage dropping below 300V threshold: increases risk of copper dissolution from the anode current collector.
safety_risks.mitigation: Isolate pack to prevent dendrite-induced short circuits.

warnings: Peak temperature reached 67.0°C, exceeding safe operating limits.
Thermal abuse: sustained temps above 60°C accelerate SEI layer growth and electrolyte decomposition.
Electrical stress risk: persistent current spikes up to 166.5A and voltage sagging to 280V.
Voltage dropping below 300V threshold: increases risk of copper dissolution from the anode current collector.
rejection_reasons: Peak temp 67.0C exceeds 60C separator damage threshold
